title: Traditional Moroccan Mrouzia Dessert
description: A rich and sweet Moroccan dessert made with lamb, almonds, and spices. Perfect for special occasions.

ingredients.name: Ground Lamb
ingredients.notes: Make sure to cook the lamb thoroughly.

ingredients.name: Almonds
ingredients.notes: You can also use other types of nuts like walnuts or pistachios.

ingredients.name: Dark Chocolate Chips
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount of chocolate to your liking.

ingredients.name: Coconut Flakes
ingredients.notes: Toasted coconut flakes can add a nice texture.

ingredients.name: Cinnamon
ingredients.notes: Adjust the amount of cinnamon to your taste.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grated ginger can add a nice warmth to the dessert.

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount of honey to your taste.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a pinch of salt to balance the sweetness.

ingredients.name: Unsweetened Almond Milk
ingredients.notes: You can use other types of milk like coconut milk or soy milk.

ingredients.name: Pistachios
ingredients.notes: Chopped pistachios can add a nice crunch to the dessert.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground lamb, almonds, dark chocolate chips, coconut flakes, cinnamon, ginger, honey, salt, and pistachios. Mix well until everything is fully incorporated.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and smooth out the top.
Bake the mixture in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until it's lightly golden brown.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Pour the unsweetened almond milk over the top of the mixture and let it sit for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid is fully absorbed.
Serve the mrouzia warm or at room temperature. You can garnish with additional almonds or pistachios if desired.
